title: 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple Box Office Hits Major Global Milestone
text: After two weeks in theatrical release, the horror sequel has accumulated $53.6 million globally, comprising $23.6 million domestically and $30 million internationally. This surpasses the $50 million worldwide benchmark. Despite reaching this milestone, the film has not yet recouped its $63 million production budget. The underperformance raises concerns about the planned trilogy conclusion. The article explains that the trilogy's creative team have been open about the fact that the first two movies need to perform well at the box office in order for the third one to become a reality. This contrasts sharply with the previous installment, which grossed approximately $150 million worldwide against its $60 million budget. While the sequel received positive critical reception, its box office trajectory may impact Sony's decision regarding the third film's greenlight status and future production.
